title: Symplectic integrators for conservative systems
description: Symplectic integrators preserve a system's energy structure over long times, unlike generic methods that drift.
body: Use when simulating a conservative physical system over long durations. Do not use a generic high-order method expecting energy conservation, since most integrators add or remove energy over time. Actions: identify whether the system is conservative, choose a symplectic integrator like leapfrog for it, and verify energy stays bounded over a long run. Validation signals include energy oscillating around a constant rather than drifting and bounded long-term behavior. Risk boundary: a non-symplectic method on a conservative system produces qualitatively wrong long-term dynamics, so flag steady energy drift as a method-choice defect.
